event: Worsening of SDH with mass effect and 2 mm midline shift
Expansion of hematoma to 15 mm with increased mass effect and 8 mm midline shift
Burr holes for evacuation of SDH and placement of subdural drain
Headache improved SDH decreased in size
Strange sensation in right arm feeling of not controlling arm
Discharged to long-term acute care hospital
Prominence of anterior temporal branch of right middle cerebral artery
Early rapid shunting of blood through cortical vein to superior sagittal sinus
Embolization of AVM using N-butyl-cyanoacrylate glue
Removed to operating room for resection of AVM
Separated from surrounding brain parenchyma using microdissection techniques
Feeding arteries draining vein coagulated using bipolar electrocautery and resected
Intraoperative Doppler ultrasound indicated no arterial flow in draining vein
Postoperative angiography showed no residual AVM
Spontaneous antigravity movement of right upper extremity
